publicationBoundary: Public-region reference evidence only. No precise fishing location, access route, sensitive species location, live condition, legal conclusion, or catch prediction is created by these records.

records.title: National Park Service fishing regulations framework

applicability.scope: Regulation hierarchy and official-verification questions for fishing in National Park units

evidence.claim: Park fishing can be governed by servicewide rules, non-conflicting state rules, park-specific rules, and superintendent restrictions.
evidence.limitations: The applicable current rule set must be checked for the specific park and date.

evidence.claim: Temporary or emergency restrictions may be issued by park authorities.
evidence.limitations: This record does not state whether any closure is active now.

records.title: North Cascades National Park fishing regulations
records.sourceAuthority: U.S. National Park Service — North Cascades National Park

applicability.scope: Broad-region regulation handoff and current-source verification for North Cascades National Park

evidence.claim: The park directs anglers to current Washington fishing regulations and identifies park-specific regulation context.
evidence.limitations: Licensing, season, species, water-specific rules, emergency changes, and current access still require direct verification.

evidence.claim: Park jurisdiction and current park guidance are relevant to legal trip planning.
evidence.limitations: The source does not verify a particular trailhead, road, shoreline, or water access point.
